crossEventQuestion: When funding a large all-cash acquisition, should Microsoft issue new debt or equity, and what is the trade-off?
observation: For the $26.2 billion LinkedIn deal, Microsoft issued new debt and accepted ~1 percent non-GAAP EPS dilution in FY17-18 to achieve accretion by FY19; for the $68.7 billion Activision deal, the all-cash structure was approved by both boards without a stated financing lever in the same call.
invariant: Debt financing defers dilution and accelerates the path to accretion compared to equity issuance for all-cash deals.
boundaryConditions: Acquisition must become accretive within a short window (two years)
Debt issuance should be timed opportunistically during favorable market windows
Transaction must meet Microsoft's high bar for new additions

decisionAxis.decisionQuestion: Debt vs equity for all-cash M&A

conditions: board approval suggests internal due diligence complete

conditionDelta: LinkedIn was a $26.2B deal with explicit dilution targets; Activision was a $68.7B deal with board approval already in place.
actionDelta: LinkedIn used debt to defer dilution; Activision proceeded with all-cash structure without a stated financing lever in the call.
